mechanisms.name: black_ice_formation
mechanisms.description: Black ice forms when temperatures drop below freezing (≤0°C) on wet surfaces
mechanisms.physical_law: Water freezes at 0°C at standard pressure

mechanisms.name: hydroplaning
mechanisms.description: Hydroplaning occurs when water depth exceeds tire tread depth, lifting tire from pavement
mechanisms.physical_law: Hydrodynamic pressure lifts tire when water can't be displaced

mechanisms.name: crosswind_rollover
mechanisms.description: High-sided vehicles can overturn when crosswind force exceeds stability threshold
mechanisms.physical_law: Overturning moment = wind pressure × side area × height

mechanisms.name: fog_reduced_visibility
mechanisms.description: Dense fog reduces visibility, increasing stopping distance and reaction time
mechanisms.physical_law: Stopping distance = reaction distance + braking distance

mechanisms.name: wet_road_reduced_friction
mechanisms.description: Wet roads reduce tire friction, increasing stopping distance by 30-50%
mechanisms.physical_law: Friction coefficient decreases by 30-50% on wet pavement

mechanisms.name: brake_fade
mechanisms.description: Prolonged braking on descent causes brake overheating and reduced stopping power
mechanisms.physical_law: Brake friction coefficient decreases exponentially with temperature

mechanisms.name: tire_blowout_asymmetric_drag
mechanisms.description: Sudden tire deflation creates asymmetric drag, pulling vehicle toward failed tire
mechanisms.physical_law: Rolling resistance increases dramatically on failed tire

mechanisms.name: dooring
mechanisms.description: Opening car door into cyclist's path causes collision with minimal reaction time
mechanisms.physical_law: Cyclist reaction time at 20 km/h is <1 second

mechanisms.name: cargo_shift_destabilization
mechanisms.description: Improperly secured cargo shifts during maneuvers, altering the vehicle's center of gravity and causing loss of control or rollover.
mechanisms.physical_law: Conservation of momentum; Torque = Force x Distance from pivot

mechanisms.name: driver_fatigue_reaction_delay
mechanisms.description: Prolonged driving hours lead to microsleep or delayed cognitive processing, increasing reaction time for urban hazards.
mechanisms.physical_law: Human reaction time increases linearly with sleep deprivation hours

mechanisms.name: blind_spot_encroachment
mechanisms.description: Large logistics vehicles have significant 'no-zones' where smaller road users (cyclists/pedestrians) are invisible to the driver.
mechanisms.physical_law: Angle of incidence vs field of view limits

mechanisms.name: infrastructure_overload_strike
mechanisms.description: Logistics vehicles exceeding height or weight limits cause structural damage to urban infrastructure like bridges or tunnels.
mechanisms.physical_law: Normal force exceeds material yield strength upon impact

mechanisms.name: double_parking_congestion_trigger
mechanisms.description: Delivery vehicles parking in active lanes force traffic into unexpected maneuvers, creating 'near-miss' scenarios or rear-end collisions.
mechanisms.physical_law: Fluid dynamics of traffic flow: capacity drops as lanes decrease

mechanisms.name: thermal_expansion_buckling
mechanisms.description: High ambient temperatures cause road surface materials (especially rail or older asphalt) to expand and buckle, creating sudden vertical hazards.
mechanisms.physical_law: Linear thermal expansion: ΔL = αL₀ΔT

mechanisms.name: crowd_turbulence_bottleneck
mechanisms.description: During public events, high-density crowds behave like a fluid; sudden movements create 'shockwaves' that push pedestrians into the path of logistics vehicles.
mechanisms.physical_law: Lighthill-Whitham-Richards (LWR) model for flow density

mechanisms.name: unmarked_construction_excavation
mechanisms.description: Road maintenance involving open trenches or milled surfaces without proper transition wedges causes suspension failure or tire snagging.
mechanisms.physical_law: Impact force = (mass × velocity²) / stopping distance

mechanisms.name: sun_glare_disability
mechanisms.description: Low-angle sun (dawn/dusk) creates intense specular reflection off wet roads or glass buildings, causing temporary functional blindness.
mechanisms.physical_law: Lambert’s Cosine Law for luminous intensity

mechanisms.name: loose_chippings_traction_loss
mechanisms.description: New 'surface dressing' maintenance leaves loose gravel; high speeds cause these chippings to act like ball bearings, reducing friction.
mechanisms.physical_law: Rolling resistance of granular media

mechanisms.name: bridge_strike_geometry
mechanisms.description: Logistics vehicles exceeding height clearances suffer structural failure upon impact with overhead infrastructure.
mechanisms.physical_law: Inelastic collision: Rigid body geometry exceeds spatial constraints

mechanisms.name: liquid_cargo_slosh
mechanisms.description: Half-full liquid tankers experience dynamic center-of-gravity shifts during cornering, leading to unpredictable lateral forces.
mechanisms.physical_law: Fluid dynamics: Free surface effect shifting the center of gravity

mechanisms.name: milled_surface_tramlining
mechanisms.description: Road maintenance involving longitudinal grooves (milling) forces tires to follow the groove path, causing 'tramlining' and loss of steering control.
mechanisms.physical_law: Lateral force generation via tire-tread and groove interlocking

mechanisms.name: thermal_expansion_buckling
mechanisms.description: Extreme heat causes road materials to expand beyond joint capacity, resulting in vertical heaving or 'blowouts'.
mechanisms.physical_law: Linear thermal expansion: ΔL = αL₀ΔT

mechanisms.name: crowd_shockwave_turbulence
mechanisms.description: High pedestrian density at events creates shockwaves where movement at the front causes a delayed, amplified 'surge' at the back.
mechanisms.physical_law: Fluid-like flow models (LWR) for high-density crowds

mechanisms.name: sun_glare_disability
mechanisms.description: Specular reflection from low-angle sun (dawn/dusk) off wet roads or glass buildings causes temporary functional blindness.
mechanisms.physical_law: Lambert’s Cosine Law for luminous intensity and disability glare

mechanisms.name: viscous_aquaplaning_thin_film
mechanisms.description: A very thin film of water mixed with road oil creates a viscous layer that reduces friction even at low speeds.
mechanisms.physical_law: Boundary lubrication theory: Friction = f(viscosity, speed, pressure)
